answer: In 2024, the Health and Human Services Commission had the highest expenditures among top Texas agencies at $62.66 billion, accounting for 33.1% of the top 100 agencies' spending. The Texas Education Agency followed with $42.38 billion (22.4%), and the Texas Department of Transportation spent $19.32 billion (10.2%). These figures highlight significant allocations to health, education, and infrastructure in the state budget.
